rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GWENDOLYN NELICE MCGRAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/06/2021. Issues: 1) Please explain/provide evidence if the original Will has been lodged with the court. (Probate Code 8200). 2) Please file a copy of the AXA Life Insurance Policy showing the beneficiaries. 3) Notice of hearing shows notice being given to Tyrone McGraw on 12/09/2020 who's death certificate shows date of death of 03/17/2020. Please give notice to Mya McGraw and Tiara McGraw. (Probate Code 1202). 4) Sharon L. McGraw has not filed consent for beneficiary to receive conservatorship estate tangible personal property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA DAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The "Declaration" filed on 2/11/21 actually amended the petition. No detailed schedules of gains and losses on sales were submitted in support of the statutory fee/commission. This Court does not ordinarily allow travel time for court hearings as part of fees and costs. There are still small discrepancies in each heir's proposed distribution. No revised proposed order has been submitted. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SERGE JOHN WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2021. 1) The proof of service of notice of the amended report does not specify the date of mailing. 2) The verification must refer to the "amended" report including the attachment (attorney's declaration). 3) Please identify all tangible items already distributed to the beneficiaries. 4) Please provide a copy of the escrow closing statement for the sale of the 22nd Avenue property. If the estate received net proceeds exceeding the FDIC limit, please provide a proof that the excess funds are held at a different institution. The Court does not approve any single institution holding funds of the estate in excess of the FDIC limit. 5) If no election is contemplated by the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate, the inventory and appraisal must be corrected to exclude the surviving spouse's share of separate property and community property passing to her. See Probate Code section 13502(a)(1) including the decedent's separate property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELIPE R. VELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for counsel to discuss and respond to the court's questions about the delay in closing this estate due to failure to obtain an EIN number in a timely manner, and the desire to pay attorney fees from private funds in order to keep the real property. in addition, it is not clear whether the PR understands prior waived fees have to be paid before the property is distributed. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANK Y. CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/2021. Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of documents filed after 2/16/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. The proposed order relief does not match what is requested in the Amendment. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the updated proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBERT SUM AND ROSE SUM REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Petition For Elder Abuse Against Petitioner Steven Sum
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine how to proceed. Other issues may include (but not limited to): Should the cross-petition to seek remedies for elder abuse under the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act (EADACPA) be transferred to the Civil Department, as there is no conservatorship proceeding involving the deceased settlor required by Welf. Inst. Code section 15657.3(a) for the Probate Department's concurrent jurisdiction over the EADACPA claims? See alsoo section 15657.3(b) PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KEN F. ROYCE AND HILDA M. ROYCE 1982 TRUSTS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter was set to be pre-granted with $2,000,000.00 bond and Kurt Trutner as successor trustee shall not sell or encumber the real property located at 2901 Mariposa Street San Francisco CA without a court order. Sabrina Royce (beneficiary) appeared and objected. The court informed Ms. Royce she needs to put her objections in writing.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CECELIA HURWICH LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Accountings, For Instructions To Trustee To Allocate And Distribute Trust Assets, For Approval Of Sale Of Real Property And T
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/21. No earlier availability. Venue is not entirely clear, as the trustee does not appear to be located in California. This one petition is for three separate trusts; each separate trust, regardless of its interrelation with another trust, is a separate entity requiring a separate case number. Additionally, if a party seeks Court approval of an account or accounts, this Court will not review subsequent accountings until one is adjudicated, and the first accounting period cannot exceed two years. [LRSF 14.35 A.] If the trustee provides authority to confirm that this is the proper venue, after each separate trust is opened, the three proceedings could be consolidated for purposes of hearings. After each trust is assigned a separate case number, each pleading caption must show the cross-reference to the other related case numbers. Original financial statements are required to verify assets on hand. [LRSF 14.35 D.] A verified statement by the attorney of record or the fiduciary must be filed, certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4. [LRSF 14.60 D.] It would also be helpful if original statements or copies of appraisals confirming the beginning balances were filed. Although the accounts have not yet been reviewed, the Summary of Account for the Amended 3rd and Final Account - Occupancy Trust (page 14) appears to be incomplete. It is unclear why there are references to an amended petition (paragraph 20) and amended accountings, when this appears to be the first petition filed in these proceedings. Furthermore, the prayer refers to accounts, not amended accounts. Paragraph 25 alleges that Donal moved out of the Residence in April 2017, but paragraph 26 states that he moved on July 1, 2017. Are there written agreements by the beneficiaries regarding the proposed distributions shown in paragraphs 34 and 35? Were receipts obtained for the distributions of cash to the grandchildren and great-grandchild? No separate declarations have been filed yet, as alleged in paragraphs 42 - 44. Notice was mailed to Alan Statman at a different address from the one shown in paragraph 17 of the petition. Courtesy copies of any pleadings filed in order to address these issues, including proofs of service, and a proposed order for each separate first trust accounting or amended accounting, must be received by the assigned Examiner no later than 4/16/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE G. MONTGOMERY JR
